serverInfo.name: sap.mcp/data_builder
serverInfo.title: data_builder
serverInfo.description: MCP server for the Datasphere Data Builder API. Provides CRUD operations for all modeling artifacts: graphical views, SQL/transformation views, analytical models, data flows, dimension views, fact views, and ER models.

tools.name: list_spaces
tools.title: list_spaces
tools.description: List all Datasphere spaces the current user has access to. Returns space IDs, names, and descriptions. Always call this first to resolve the target space ID before any entity operation.

tools.name: get_space
tools.title: get_space
tools.description: Get detailed information about a single Datasphere space by its ID, including space type, storage quota, assigned users, and allowed connections.

tools.name: list_entities
tools.title: list_entities
tools.description: List entities (views, tables, analytical models, data flows, ER models, etc.) inside a Datasphere space. Accepts filters: spaceId (required), type (optional: 'view','analyticModel','dataFlow','erModel','taskChain'), name prefix, $top/$skip for pagination. Returns technical name, type, status, and deployment state.

tools.name: get_entity
tools.title: get_entity
tools.description: Retrieve the full JSON definition of a single entity (view, analytical model, data flow, etc.) from a Datasphere space. Required params: spaceId, technicalName. Returns the full entity payload including columns, sources, joins, measures, dimensions, and SQL script where applicable.

tools.name: get_entity_status
tools.title: get_entity_status
tools.description: Get the current deployment status of an entity in a Datasphere space. Returns status: DEPLOYED, DESIGN_TIME_ONLY, ERROR, DEPLOYING. Required params: spaceId, technicalName.

tools.name: validate_entity
tools.title: validate_entity
tools.description: Validate an entity definition (view, data flow, analytical model, etc.) in a Datasphere space without persisting or deploying it. Returns validation errors, warnings, and a pass/fail result. Required params: spaceId, entityPayload (JSON). Use before create/update to catch schema errors early.

tools.name: create_graphical_view
tools.title: create_graphical_view
tools.description: Create a new Graphical View in a Datasphere space using a visual node-based definition. The payload must include: spaceId, technicalName, label (business name), semanticUsage ('RELATIONAL_DATASET', 'FACT', 'DIMENSION', 'HIERARCHY'), and a 'definition' object describing the source nodes, join conditions, projection (selected columns), filter conditions, aggregation nodes (GROUP BY + measures), and any calculated columns (formula expressions). Joins support: INNER, LEFT_OUTER, RIGHT_OUTER, FULL_OUTER, CROSS. Column expressions support: arithmetic, string functions, date functions, CASE WHEN, IF-THEN-ELSE, nested lookups.

tools.name: update_graphical_view
tools.title: update_graphical_view
tools.description: Update an existing Graphical View definition in a Datasphere space. Required params: spaceId, technicalName, full updated definition payload. The entity must already exist; use get_entity to retrieve the current definition before modifying. Supports adding/removing source nodes, changing join types, updating projection columns, adding calculated columns, and changing aggregation logic.

tools.name: create_sql_view
tools.title: create_sql_view
tools.description: Create a new SQL View (also called Transformation View) in a Datasphere space using a SQL SELECT statement. Required params: spaceId, technicalName, label, sqlScript (a valid HANA SQL SELECT statement). The SQL may include: CTEs (WITH clause), projections (SELECT col1, col2), calculated columns (col * 2 AS doubled), aggregations (GROUP BY + SUM/COUNT/AVG/MIN/MAX/STDEV), HAVING clause, window functions (ROW_NUMBER/RANK/DENSE_RANK/SUM OVER PARTITION), CASE WHEN expressions, scalar subqueries, UNION/INTERSECT/EXCEPT, and input parameters ($$PARAM_NAME$$). Optional: semanticUsage, input parameters definition, output column metadata. Best for: multi-step CTEs, reuse of complex SQL logic, advanced window analytics, Python UDF references.

tools.name: update_sql_view
tools.title: update_sql_view
tools.description: Update the SQL script and metadata of an existing SQL View in a Datasphere space. Required params: spaceId, technicalName, updated sqlScript and/or metadata. Triggers re-validation of the SQL. The view must be re-deployed after update using deploy_entity.

tools.name: create_analytical_model
tools.title: create_analytical_model
tools.description: Create a new Analytical Model in a Datasphere space. This is a star-schema style artifact primarily used for SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) story consumption. Required params: spaceId, technicalName, label, factSource (technicalName of the fact view or relational dataset), dimensionAssociations (list of dimension views with join keys), measures (list with aggregation: SUM/COUNT/MIN/MAX/AVG), calculatedMeasures (formula-based measures, e.g. 'Revenue / Quantity'), restrictedMeasures (measures with hard-coded filter conditions), variables (input parameters for report-time filtering), and hierarchies (optional, for drill-down). The analytical model does NOT store data; it is a semantic layer on top of existing views.

tools.name: update_analytical_model
tools.title: update_analytical_model
tools.description: Update an existing Analytical Model in a Datasphere space. Supports adding/removing dimensions, updating measures, adding calculated measures, updating variable definitions, and changing fact source. Required params: spaceId, technicalName, updated model payload.

tools.name: create_data_flow
tools.title: create_data_flow
tools.description: Create a new Data Flow in a Datasphere space. Data Flows are ETL/ELT pipelines that read from source entities, apply transformations, and write to a target local table. Required params: spaceId, technicalName, label, sourceEntity (technicalName + spaceId of source), targetEntity (technicalName of local table to write to), loadType ('INITIAL' for full load, 'DELTA' for incremental). Optional transformation operators: Projection (select/rename columns), Filter (WHERE condition), Aggregation (GROUP BY + aggregate columns), Join (combine two source branches), Union (combine multiple sources of same structure), Script (SQLScript or Python). Delta settings: deltaColumn (timestamp/sequence column), deltaMode ('UPSERT', 'APPEND', 'DELETE_INSERT').

tools.name: update_data_flow
tools.title: update_data_flow
tools.description: Update an existing Data Flow definition in a Datasphere space. Supports changing source, target, transformation operators, delta settings, and adding/removing transformation nodes. Required params: spaceId, technicalName, updated flow payload.

tools.name: create_dimension_view
tools.title: create_dimension_view
tools.description: Create a new Dimension View in a Datasphere space. A Dimension View is a Graphical or SQL View with semanticUsage='DIMENSION'. It defines master data attributes for slicing and filtering in Analytical Models and SAC stories. Required params: spaceId, technicalName, label, key columns (one or more columns forming the unique key), attribute columns (descriptive columns), optional text column (human-readable label for each key value), optional hierarchies (parent-child or level-based for drill-down). Text association: link to a text entity that provides locale-aware descriptions.

tools.name: create_fact_view
tools.title: create_fact_view
tools.description: Create a new Fact View in a Datasphere space. A Fact View is a Graphical or SQL View with semanticUsage='FACT'. It contains the quantitative measures (e.g. sales amount, quantity, cost) and foreign keys to Dimension Views. Required params: spaceId, technicalName, label, key columns, measure columns (with declared aggregation: SUM/COUNT/MIN/MAX/AVG), dimension associations (foreign key columns that join to Dimension Views). The combination of Fact View + Dimension Views forms the star schema consumed by Analytical Models.

tools.name: create_er_model
tools.title: create_er_model
tools.description: Create a new ER Model in a Datasphere space. ER Models are documentation-only artifacts that visually display the relationships (associations) between entities (tables, views) without storing or transforming data. Required params: spaceId, technicalName, label, entities (list of technicalNames to include), associations (source entity, target entity, join columns, cardinality: ONE_TO_ONE/ONE_TO_MANY/MANY_TO_ONE/MANY_TO_MANY). Useful for: data governance documentation, schema exploration, onboarding, and lineage understanding.

tools.name: update_er_model
tools.title: update_er_model
tools.description: Update an existing ER Model in a Datasphere space. Supports adding/removing entities and associations. Required params: spaceId, technicalName, updated associations list.

tools.name: create_task_chain
tools.title: create_task_chain
tools.description: Create a new Task Chain in a Datasphere space. Task Chains orchestrate and schedule the sequential or parallel execution of Data Flows and other tasks. Required params: spaceId, technicalName, label, tasks (ordered list of entity references with type 'dataFlow'), schedule (optional cron expression). Supports conditional branching: 'onSuccess', 'onFailure' routing.

tools.name: delete_entity
tools.title: delete_entity
tools.description: Delete an entity (view, analytical model, data flow, ER model, task chain, etc.) from a Datasphere space. Required params: spaceId, technicalName. WARNING: This action is irreversible. The entity must be undeployed first (status DESIGN_TIME_ONLY) before it can be deleted. Always request user approval before calling this tool.

tools.name: deploy_entity
tools.title: deploy_entity
tools.description: Deploy (activate/publish) an entity to the Datasphere HANA Cloud database, making it queryable and accessible to consumers. Required params: spaceId, technicalName. Triggers compilation, validation, and physical creation of the database object. Returns a jobId; call get_deploy_job_status to poll for completion. Prerequisite: the entity definition must pass validation. Deployment is required after create or update before consumers can access the new/changed view.

tools.name: undeploy_entity
tools.title: undeploy_entity
tools.description: Undeploy an entity from Datasphere HANA Cloud, removing the physical database object but keeping the design-time definition. Required params: spaceId, technicalName. After undeployment the entity returns to DESIGN_TIME_ONLY status. Required before delete_entity.

tools.name: get_deploy_job_status
tools.title: get_deploy_job_status
tools.description: Poll the status of an asynchronous deploy or undeploy job in Datasphere. Required params: spaceId, jobId (returned by deploy_entity/undeploy_entity). Returns: status (RUNNING, COMPLETED, FAILED), progress percentage, and error details if failed.

tools.name: list_entity_impact
tools.title: list_entity_impact
tools.description: Get the impact analysis for an entity: which other views, analytical models, or data flows depend on it. Required params: spaceId, technicalName. Returns a list of dependent entities with their type and deployment status. Use before delete_entity or schema changes to understand downstream impact.

tools.name: list_entity_lineage
tools.title: list_entity_lineage
tools.description: Get the data lineage for an entity: which source tables and views it depends on, transitively. Required params: spaceId, technicalName. Returns an upstream lineage tree with entity types, spaces, and connection references for cross-space or remote sources.

tools.name: get_column_suggestions
tools.title: get_column_suggestions
tools.description: Get column name and type suggestions for a new view based on the specified source entities. Required params: spaceId, sourceEntities (list of technicalNames). Returns available columns with data types, semantic types (dimension/measure/text/key), and suggested aggregations for numeric columns. Use in Step 3 of the modeling workflow to populate proposed schemas without guessing column names.
